function: sys.ms_round(x double, prc int, trunc int)
sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, value DOUBLE, description VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, description) VALUES (1, 1.2359, 'First entry'), (2, 2.7891, 'Second entry'), (3, NULL, 'Third entry'), (4, -10.0001, 'Negative entry');
SELECT id, sys.ms_round(value + 2.5, 2, 0) AS rounded_value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;

SELECT t1.id, sys.ms_round(SUM(t2.value) + 1.1234, 2, 1) AS aggregated_rounded_value FROM test_table t1 JOIN test_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t2.value < 0 GROUP BY t1.id HAVING sys.ms_round(SUM(t2.value), 1, 1) < -10;
SELECT id, sys.ms_round(sys.ms_round(value, 2, 0) * 3 - sys.ms_round(0.1234, 3, 2), 2, 1) AS complex_calculation FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL ORDER BY complex_calculation;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, sys.ms_round(value, 2, 1) AS rounded_value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT t1.id, sys.ms_round(t2.rounded_value + CAST('1' AS DOUBLE), 2, 0) FROM temp_table t1 JOIN temp_table t2 ON t1.id = t2.id WHERE t1.rounded_value IS NOT NULL;
INSERT INTO temp_table (id, rounded_value) VALUES (5, sys.ms_round(NULL, 2, 0)), (6, sys.ms_round(0, 2, 1));
SELECT MAX(sys.ms_round(value + CAST(NULL AS DOUBLE), 2, 0)) FROM test_table;
SELECT t1.id FROM test_table t1 WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table t2 WHERE t2.value + sys.ms_round(t1.value, 2, 0) > 5);
